description: A hot key expires and thousands of concurrent requests all miss and rebuild from the source at once, overloading it.
body: Failure mode: a popular key's TTL fires; every in-flight request misses simultaneously and hits the source database to rebuild, spiking source load and sometimes cascading into an outage (thundering herd). Detection signals: periodic source load spikes aligned with TTL boundaries; many identical source queries in a burst; shared expiry across keys. Do not give hot keys plain synchronized TTLs. Safer fallback: add random jitter to TTLs, use single-flight or a short lock so one request rebuilds while others wait, and optionally serve a slightly stale value during rebuild.
